notes: All candidates completed with model-backed analysis.

candidates.title: chore(release): prepare v0.0.14
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream only prepares its v0.0.14 release by bumping package versions in package manifests and `bun.lock` from `0.0.13` to `0.0.14`.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code uses its own package identities and release line, so this version-only bookkeeping would not change desktop, server, or contracts behavior in the fork.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local package metadata is already on an independent scheme: `apps/desktop/package.json`, `apps/server/package.json`, and `packages/contracts/package.json` are all `0.1.5` under `@samscode/*`, and `bun.lock` records additional fork-specific workspaces like `apps/cli` and `apps/desktop-renderer` instead of upstream `apps/web`. Because the upstream commit only advances T3's release numbers and lockfile entries for its own workspace layout, applying it would be incorrect fork-version churn rather than a functional improvement.

candidates.title: Preserve git action progress until HTTP completion (#1388)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream keeps git progress timing state intact when websocket completion events arrive so the loading toast retains its "Running for Xs" description until the HTTP success or error handler replaces it.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code also shows git action progress toasts, but they are driven by a single request lifecycle rather than separate websocket progress completion events plus a later HTTP response.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `GitActionsControl` owns the entire git action toast lifecycle inside `runGitActionWithToast`, starting a loading toast, advancing staged labels with `setInterval`, and only converting that same toast to success or error after awaiting `runImmediateGitActionMutation.mutateAsync` (`apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/GitActionsControl.tsx:315-446`). That mutation is just a single `api.git.runStackedAction` request/response (`apps/desktop-renderer/src/lib/gitReactQuery.ts:112-143`, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/wsNativeApi.ts:232-245`), and the server handles `gitRunStackedAction` by directly returning `gitManager.runStackedAction(body)` in the websocket request handler (`apps/server/src/wsServer.ts:871-873`). There is no separate git progress push channel, `activeGitActionProgressRef`, or `action_finished`/`action_failed` event gap matching upstream's race, so this upstream fix does not map to the fork's architecture.

candidates.title: Persist provider-aware model selections (#1371)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream replaces bare project/thread model fields and turn-start provider-plus-model fields with a persisted provider-aware model selection object across orchestration contracts, projections, and persistence.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already sends explicit provider choice at turn start and persists provider runtime bindings separately, while its stored project/thread model fields remain intentionally slug-only.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local contracts and projections are built around separate concerns: `packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts` still defines `project.defaultModel`, `thread.model`, and `thread.turn.start` with separate `provider`, `model`, and `modelOptions`; persistence stores only `projection_projects.default_model` and `projection_threads.model` in `apps/server/src/persistence/Layers/ProjectionProjects.ts` and `apps/server/src/persistence/Layers/ProjectionThreads.ts`; actual provider recovery is handled by the separate provider runtime binding with persisted `provider_name` in `apps/server/src/persistence/Layers/ProviderSessionRuntime.ts` and `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderService.ts`. On the renderer side, turns already dispatch explicit `provider: selectedProvider` plus `model` in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx`, and when no live session exists the UI intentionally infers provider from disjoint local model catalogs via `inferProviderForModel` in `packages/shared/src/model.ts`. Because Sam's Code only exposes non-overlapping Codex and Claude model slugs in `packages/contracts/src/model.ts`, the upstream `modelSelection` migration would be a large contract/persistence rewrite with little reliability gain for this fork's actual restart and routing path.

candidates.title: Pick sidebar fallback thread after delete (#1395)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds a sidebar delete fallback helper so deleting the active thread navigates to the top remaining thread from that thread's own project, using the configured sidebar sort order and excluding other threads deleted in the same action.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same active-thread delete flow, multi-delete support, and user-configurable sidebar thread sorting, so this change would keep post-delete navigation consistent with the visible sidebar instead of jumping arbitrarily.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local delete handling in apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/Sidebar.tsx still computes fallback with `threads.find((entry) => entry.id !== threadId && !allDeletedIds.has(entry.id))`, which ignores project boundaries and ignores `appSettings.sidebarThreadSortOrder`. That conflicts with the local sidebar model in the same file, where visible threads are explicitly `sortThreadsForSidebar(threads.filter((thread) => thread.projectId === activeProject.id), appSettings.sidebarThreadSortOrder)`. Sam's Code is even more project-sensitive than upstream because the sidebar is filtered to one active project at a time, and `activeProjectId` is only changed manually via `setActiveProject` in ProjectSelector or initial load, not derived from route changes. As written, deleting the current thread can navigate to a thread from another project while the sidebar stays focused on the previous project. The upstream fallback helper directly addresses that mismatch and should be adapted here.

candidates.title: fix(security): send sensitive config over a bootstrap fd (#1398)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream stops passing desktop backend secrets through process environment and command-line visible config by introducing a one-shot bootstrap file descriptor for server startup and by moving renderer websocket URL lookup from preload env access to an IPC call into the Electron main process.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same Electron-main-launches-server-child architecture, and it currently propagates the desktop auth token and websocket URL through environment variables in both packaged and dev flows, so the same secret exposure risk exists here.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local desktop startup still injects `SAMSCODE_AUTH_TOKEN` directly into the backend child env in `apps/desktop/src/main.ts` via `backendEnv()` and also stores the full `ws://127.0.0.1:.../?token=...` URL in `process.env.SAMSCODE_DESKTOP_WS_URL` during bootstrap (`apps/desktop/src/main.ts`, around lines 993-1001 and 1536-1539). Preload then exposes that env var verbatim with `getWsUrl: () => wsUrl` in `apps/desktop/src/preload.ts:20-23`, and the renderer consumes it in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/store.ts:197-203` and `apps/desktop-renderer/src/wsTransport.ts:79-82`. On the server side, `apps/server/src/main.ts` only reads auth/config from CLI and env (`SAMSCODE_AUTH_TOKEN`, `SAMSCODE_PORT`, `SAMSCODE_MODE`) and there is no local support for `SAMSCODE_BOOTSTRAP_FD`, `bootstrap-fd`, or `readBootstrapEnvelope` anywhere in the repo. `scripts/dev-runner.ts:144-159` also still exports `SAMSCODE_PORT`, `VITE_WS_URL`, and `SAMSCODE_AUTH_TOKEN` into the desktop dev environment. That is a direct match for the upstream security intent, so this change should be adapted rather than ignored.

candidates.title: docs: properly linked CLAUDE.md to AGENTS.md (#1328)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream fixes the `CLAUDE.md` link by correcting the symlink payload so it points cleanly to `AGENTS.md` without a trailing newline.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already carries `CLAUDE.md` as a plain tracked file that contains `AGENTS.md`, so the upstream symlink-specific fix does not add value to this fork.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local evidence shows `CLAUDE.md` is tracked as mode `100644` (`git ls-files -s -- CLAUDE.md`), not upstream's symlink mode `120000`, and `git log --follow -- CLAUDE.md` shows it was introduced that way in the fork's initial commit. The upstream patch only fixes symlink newline semantics; applying that exact change does not match the fork's regular-file approach.

candidates.title: fix(web): prevent provider model submenu overlap (#1403)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds spacing between the provider menu item and its model submenu by passing `sideOffset={4}` to the submenu popup, and adds a browser test that verifies the submenu opens with a visible gap instead of overlapping the parent menu.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same nested provider/model picker in the desktop renderer, so this UI fix would improve the live multi-provider menu and reduce hover/collision issues when switching between Codex and Claude models.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/chat/ProviderModelPicker.tsx` still renders `<MenuSubPopup className="[--available-height:min(24rem,70vh)]">` with no offset, and local `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ui/menu.tsx` defines `MenuSubPopup` with `sideOffset = 0`, so the fork has not absorbed the gap fix. This submenu path is active, not dead code, because `apps/desktop-renderer/src/session-logic.ts` exposes both `codex` and `claudeAgent`, and `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx` mounts `ProviderModelPicker` in the main composer header. The upstream intent maps directly to the fork's desktop renderer architecture.

candidates.title: feat(git): Add Claude as a TextGenerationProvider (#1323)
candidates.summary:
Co-authored-by: keyzou <keyzou@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Julius Marminge <julius0216@outlook.com>
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream makes git text generation provider-aware so commit messages, PR content, and branch names can be generated through Claude as well as Codex instead of always going through the Codex CLI.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already has first-class Claude provider support and shared Claude model metadata, but its git automation path is still Codex-only. Adapting this upstream intent would close that gap and make git generation consistent with the fork's broader multi-provider architecture.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local evidence shows this is not already present and is a good fit for the fork: apps/server/src/serverLayers.ts binds const textGenerationLayer = CodexTextGenerationLive, packages/contracts/src/git.ts exposes only a textGenerationModel string with a Codex default, and apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx builds gitTextGenerationModelOptions from getAppModelOptions("codex", ...). At the same time, the fork already models Claude providers and options in packages/contracts/src/model.ts and already routes speech cleanup by inferred provider in apps/server/src/speechToText/transcriptCleanup.ts, so provider-aware git text generation matches existing fork architecture and should be adapted rather than ignored.

candidates.title: Add copy action to error toast messages (#1416)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds an inline copy control for error toast messages and makes toast descriptions selectable text.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code uses the same shared toast surface in the desktop renderer for operational failures from git actions, desktop updates, and upstream-sync flows, so copying exact error text would help debugging and support without changing toast semantics.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ui/toast.tsx` still renders toast descriptions without any copy affordance, and the toast root applies `select-none` while the description classes at lines 292 and 381 do not restore text selection. At the same time, several local flows send raw `error.message` strings into toast descriptions, including `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/CommitPanel.tsx:108-110` and `:144-146`, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/DesktopUpdateNotifications.tsx:118-120` and `:140-142`, and `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/UpstreamSyncSection.tsx:299-301` and `:435-438`. The renderer already has a reusable clipboard hook and copy-button pattern in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts` and `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/chat/MessageCopyButton.tsx`, so this upstream intent matches the fork architecture and fills a current UX gap rather than duplicating existing behavior.

candidates.title: fix(desktop): prevent git actions and terminal toggle from overlapping (#1423)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds `className="shrink-0"` to the git action button group to stop the horizontal git actions strip from collapsing into the terminal toggle.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code moved terminal and git controls into a dropdown menu, so the specific top-bar overlap the upstream patch fixes does not occur in the fork.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.tsx` passes `onToggleTerminal` into `HamburgerMenu`, and `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/HamburgerMenu.tsx` renders the terminal toggle as a standalone menu item while `GitActionsControl` is rendered later in a separate `Git` section inside the dropdown. In `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/GitActionsControl.tsx`, the `Group aria-label="Git actions"` is therefore no longer sharing a horizontal header row with the terminal control, so the upstream `shrink-0` change does not match the fork's current UI architecture.

candidates.title: fix(web): clear composer border after picker dismissal (#1382)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream changes the composer container border highlight from `focus-within:border-ring/45` to `has-focus-visible:border-ring/45` so dismissing a picker does not leave the composer looking focused.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same composer wrapper class in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx` and the same embedded picker pattern, so the fix would remove a stale focus border in the desktop renderer too.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx` still uses `focus-within:border-ring/45` on the composer shell at line 3643, and that shell contains `ProviderModelPicker` plus provider traits pickers at lines 3797 and 3823. Those pickers are implemented as shared `Menu` triggers in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/chat/ProviderModelPicker.tsx`, `CodexTraitsPicker.tsx`, and `ClaudeTraitsPicker.tsx`, so picker dismissal can keep DOM focus inside the composer even when visible focus should be cleared. The fork already uses `has-focus-visible` for comparable input chrome in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ui/input.tsx` and `textarea.tsx`, which makes the composer wrapper an inconsistent outlier rather than a deliberate desktop-specific behavior.

candidates.title: fix(web): add pointer cursor to composer send button (#1415)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds explicit pointer affordance and disabled pointer-event blocking to the circular composer send button in `ChatView` by adding `enabled:cursor-pointer` and `disabled:pointer-events-none` to its class list.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same circular raw submit button in the desktop renderer composer, and applying this would align its default send control with the rest of the fork's clickable button behavior.
candidates.recommendedReason: `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx` still has the raw composer submit button at lines 4057-4062 with the pre-fix class string and no pointer cursor or disabled pointer-event handling, while the shared `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ui/button.tsx` primitive already standardizes `cursor-pointer` and `disabled:pointer-events-none` for other controls. This fork is Electron-based but still mouse-driven, and most nearby actions in `ChatView` already use either that shared `Button` primitive or explicit `cursor-pointer`, so this upstream fix cleanly improves consistency for the one send path that currently bypasses those affordances.

candidates.title: Make server settings authoritative for runtime behavior (#1421)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds a persisted server-settings source of truth and routes runtime behavior through it, so provider startup, health checks, and text-generation paths all use the same server-owned CLI settings instead of ad hoc inputs.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already exposes CLI path settings, but runtime consumers are split between renderer-owned settings and server hardcoded defaults. Adopting the upstream intent would make CLI selection and background behavior more predictable.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local settings are fragmented today: apps/desktop-renderer/src/appSettings.ts stores codexBinaryPath and codexHomePath, apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx and apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/UpstreamSyncSection.tsx pass them as providerOptions, and apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderService.ts persists those options for session recovery, but apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderHealth.ts and apps/server/src/git/Layers/CodexTextGeneration.ts still hardcode plain codex and ignore that configuration. packages/contracts/src/server.ts also has no server-side settings surface yet, so the upstream change is not already present; adapting it would eliminate a concrete runtime-consistency gap in the fork.

candidates.title: Add Claude context window selection support (#1422)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds Claude context-window selection support by extending Claude model options, resolving the API model id from the logical Claude selection, and wiring the choice through the server and composer UI.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already has Claude-specific trait controls and a context-window meter, but it cannot currently store or dispatch a Claude context-window choice.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local evidence shows the feature is missing end to end: `packages/contracts/src/model.ts` defines `ClaudeModelOptions` with only `thinking`, `effort`, and `fastMode`; `packages/shared/src/model.ts` normalizes only those fields; and `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts` forwards only `model`, `effort`, `thinking`, `fastMode`, and `maxThinkingTokens` to the Claude SDK. At the same time, the fork already has the right architecture to benefit from this change: composer provider options are persisted and dispatched through `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/chat/composerProviderRegistry.tsx`, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/composerDraftStore.ts`, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx`, and `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderService.ts`, and the UI already surfaces Claude context-window telemetry via `apps/desktop-renderer/src/lib/contextWindow.ts`. Adding a real `contextWindow` option would complete an existing UX path rather than introduce an unrelated upstream pattern.

candidates.title: Add VS Code Insiders and VSCodium to Open In editor picker (#1392)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds VS Code Insiders and VSCodium to the shared editor catalog, teaches editor launch logic to treat them like VS Code for "--goto", and exposes them in the Open In UI/tests.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already has a shared editor contract, server-side editor discovery, and renderer-side preferred-editor persistence, so supporting "code-insiders" and "codium" would make the existing open-in flow work for users who do not have stable VS Code installed.
candidates.recommendedReason: This is not already present locally: `packages/contracts/src/editor.ts` only defines `cursor`, `vscode`, `zed`, `antigravity`, and `file-manager`; `apps/server/src/open.ts` only applies `--goto` to `cursor` and `vscode`; and both `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/chat/OpenInPicker.tsx` and the mounted `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/HamburgerMenu.tsx` hardcode the same reduced editor list. The fork already resolves the preferred editor from shared `EDITORS` in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/editorPreferences.ts`, so adding `vscode-insiders` and `vscodium` fits the existing architecture cleanly, but it should be adapted to the fork's hamburger-menu entry point rather than copied literally from upstream's web ChatView tests.

candidates.title: fix(web): improve chat header badge and title flex distribution (#1309)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adjusts the web chat header so the title/project badge can shrink correctly and action-button labels only appear at much wider container sizes.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code uses a different desktop titlebar composition, so the upstream flex-and-badge fix does not map cleanly to the fork's renderer.
candidates.recommendedReason: The local header at apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/chat/ChatHeader.tsx is a JetBrains-style titlebar that renders AppLogo, HamburgerMenu, ProjectSelector, HeaderBranchSelector, ProjectScriptsControl, a standalone play button, and Settings. It does not render the upstream title/badge layout at all: activeThreadTitle and activeProjectName are declared props but are unused in the component body. The upstream patch's main fix is therefore irrelevant. Also, the local renderer still has @sm/header-actions classes inside apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/GitActionsControl.tsx, apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ProjectScriptsControl.tsx, and apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/chat/OpenInPicker.tsx, but there is no local @container/header-actions usage in apps/desktop-renderer/src, so upstream's container-query breakpoint changes would be a partial web-specific transplant rather than a coherent fork improvement.

candidates.title: Support `gh pr checkout` pull request references (#1457)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream teaches pull-request entry points to accept `gh pr checkout ...`, keeps the synthetic PR checkout item visible in branch search results, and runs the project setup script after preparing a pull-request worktree thread.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already has matching desktop-renderer and server primitives for PR resolution/preparation, branch-toolbar PR checkout, and `runOnWorktreeCreate` project scripts, so this change maps directly onto existing fork architecture and closes real behavior gaps.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `parsePullRequestReference` only accepts GitHub PR URLs and numeric refs in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/pullRequestReference.ts:1-18`, and the dialog copy still advertises only URL/`123`/`#123` in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/PullRequestThreadDialog.tsx:156`, `:158`, and `:194`. The branch selector already creates a synthetic checkout item, but `filteredBranchPickerItems` in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/BranchToolbarBranchSelector.tsx:128-153` only force-keeps the create-branch item, so a query like `gh pr checkout 1359` would filter the PR item out even after parser support lands. Separately, PR thread preparation currently stops at `openOrReuseProjectDraftThread` in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx:584-592`, while the normal new-worktree send path already honors `setupProjectScript` and `runProjectScript` in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx:2679-2701`. Because Sam's Code already exposes `git.resolvePullRequest`/`git.preparePullRequestThread` and `runOnWorktreeCreate` script semantics, the upstream change fills missing behavior rather than conflicting with fork design.

candidates.title: fix(claude): avoid resetting the Claude model on every turn (#1466)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream makes the Claude adapter remember the effective API model for a session so sendTurn only calls setModel when that effective model actually changes, and it adds regression coverage for both unchanged and changed model cases.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code also supports in-session Claude model switching and currently forwards the selected model on every turn, so carrying over this guard would remove redundant Claude SDK model resets and keep runtime session state aligned with the active model.
candidates.recommendedReason: The local fork still has the exact failure mode. apps/server/src/orchestration/Layers/ProviderCommandReactor.ts:392 and :398 compute modelForTurn and pass it into every providerService.sendTurn call, while apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts:2713-2716 unconditionally calls context.query.setModel(input.model) whenever a model is present. There is no currentApiModelId tracking in the local ClaudeSessionContext, and sendTurn does not refresh context.session.model after an in-session change even though ProviderService exposes session.model in runtime payloads at apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderService.ts:90-99. Sam's Code does not have the upstream guard yet, so repeated Claude turns with the same model will keep reissuing setModel and can leave surfaced session metadata stale after a model switch.

candidates.title: Add Effect.fn refactor checklist (#1476)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream mechanically converts Claude adapter helpers from wrapper-style "return Effect.gen(...)" functions to "Effect.fn(...)" and adds a generated "docs/effect-fn-checklist.md" for a broader refactor campaign.
candidates.forkValueSummary: This would only provide style-level maintainability value if Sam's Code chooses to adopt an "Effect.fn" convention across the repo; it does not add runtime behavior or reliability improvements by itself.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local Claude adapter still uses the wrapper pattern in the same spots upstream touched: "buildUserMessageEffect" at apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts:528 and "makeClaudeAdapter" at apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts:887 both return "Effect.gen(function* ...)" today. A repo-wide search in apps/server/src, apps/desktop, apps/desktop-renderer, and packages found no existing "Effect.fn(" usage, so applying this single upstream refactor would introduce a new Effect style in one file only. The added upstream docs file is a generated checklist tied to upstream paths, counts, and line numbers, so it would be stale for this fork immediately and does not map to Sam's Code architecture as maintained documentation.

candidates.title: fix(threads): Keep active-turn runtime errors from ending sessions (#1261)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream stops classifying Codex `process/stderr` lines as provider errors and emits them as notifications instead, then maps those notifications to `runtime.warning` rather than `runtime.error`.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same stderr classification point and an even stronger need for this downgrade because local orchestration converts provider runtime errors into thread session error state during active turns.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local code matches the upstream failure mode. In `apps/server/src/codexAppServerManager.ts`, classified stderr lines still call `emitErrorEvent(context, "process/stderr", ...)` at line 970, and `emitErrorEvent` emits `kind: "error"` around lines 1247-1255. In `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/CodexAdapter.ts`, any `event.kind === "error"` is mapped to `type: "runtime.error"` around lines 528-540, while only selected notification methods such as `windows/worldWritableWarning` are downgraded to `runtime.warning` around lines 1170-1178. That matters in this fork because `apps/server/src/orchestration/Layers/ProviderRuntimeIngestion.ts` handles `runtime.error` by dispatching `thread.session.set` with `status: "error"` and `lastError` around lines 1150-1168. `packages/contracts/src/provider.ts` already supports `kind: "notification"` at lines 94-109, so the upstream change fits the local event model and would prevent non-fatal Codex stderr during a turn from incorrectly pushing the session into error state.

candidates.title: Improve drain semantics via STM in `DrainableWorker` (#1474)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream makes `DrainableWorker` drain semantics deterministic with STM-backed tracking and updates async callback sites/tests to use captured Effect runtimes instead of ambient `runPromise`/`runFork` calls, alongside the related Effect beta API migration.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same shared worker abstraction and the same callback-heavy server/provider runtime boundaries, so porting the intent would improve event-pipeline determinism and callback reliability under session restarts, stream aborts, and websocket activity.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `packages/shared/src/DrainableWorker.ts` is still the older `Deferred` + `Queue` + `Ref` implementation, and that worker directly backs the fork's orchestration hot paths in `apps/server/src/orchestration/Layers/ProviderRuntimeIngestion.ts`, `ProviderCommandReactor.ts`, and `CheckpointReactor.ts`. The local server already shows the same runtime-capture concern upstream fixed: `apps/server/src/wsServer.ts` defines `runPromise = Effect.runPromiseWith(runtimeServices)` at line 639, but still uses bare `Effect.runPromise` in the HTTP handler and terminal-event subscription at lines 441 and 642. `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts` also still uses bare `Effect.runFork`/`Effect.runPromise` inside session-scoped callbacks at lines 2445, 2493, 2686, and 2695. That is a concrete match to the upstream intent. The exact patch will need adaptation because this fork is still pinned to the older `effect-smol` snapshot in `package.json`, but the change should be brought over rather than ignored.

candidates.title: Refactor Codex adapter lifecycle helpers (#1478)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream refactors the Codex adapter by naming `Effect.fn` lifecycle helpers and extracting attachment resolution, manager acquisition, and event-listener setup out of the main adapter constructor.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code could benefit from future cleanup in this area, but this exact upstream refactor is mostly structural and targets an adapter shape that has already diverged in the fork.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local Codex adapter wiring no longer matches the upstream surface this refactor was written for. Sam's Code `ProviderAdapterShape` exposes `sendTurn` and `respondToUserInput` only (`apps/server/src/provider/Services/ProviderAdapter.ts:61-77`), and local `ProviderSendTurnInput` carries `model` plus `modelOptions` (`packages/contracts/src/provider.ts:55-67`), which the adapter maps via `input.model` and `input.modelOptions.codex` (`apps/server/src/provider/Layers/CodexAdapter.ts:1321-1325`). The same file also contains fork-only autonomous-mode gated-request filtering in the event listener (`apps/server/src/provider/Layers/CodexAdapter.ts:1424-1447`). Because the upstream patch is a helper extraction around a different contract shape (`modelSelection`/`respondToRequest` era code) rather than a behavior fix, porting it here would be high-conflict refactor churn without a concrete reliability or performance gain for the fork.

candidates.title: Skip a unit test that is using a bash script on Windows (#1490)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream skips a Windows-incompatible ProviderRegistry unit test that writes a bash-based fake `codex` executable to verify PATH inheritance when `CODEX_HOME` is overridden.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already avoids that failure mode by testing Codex health probing through mocked process spawning and separate Windows-aware CLI resolution tests instead of a bash-script fixture.
candidates.recommendedReason: This fork does not have `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderRegistry.test.ts`. The equivalent Codex probe coverage is in `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderHealth.test.ts`, which drives `checkCodexProviderStatus` through a mocked `ChildProcessSpawner` and never writes a `#!/bin/sh` fake binary, and in `apps/server/src/provider/resolveCliBinary.test.ts` plus `apps/server/src/provider/resolveCliBinary.ts`, which explicitly branch for Windows `.exe` and `.cmd` handling. On this Windows workspace, `bun x vitest run src/provider/resolveCliBinary.test.ts src/provider/Layers/ProviderHealth.test.ts` passed with 40 tests green, so the upstream Windows-only skip addresses a bash-fixture problem the fork has already designed away.

candidates.title: Detect command not found when checking codex/claude CLI on Windows (#1492)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream exports Windows command-not-found detection and routes Codex and Claude CLI probes through a shared spawn helper so shell exit 9009 or "is not recognized as an internal or external command" is treated as a missing binary, not a generic probe failure.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code centralizes Codex and Claude readiness checks in one ProviderHealth layer, so this Windows detection bug currently affects both providers and can misreport a missing CLI as an installed-but-broken one.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local provider health still spawns Codex and Claude directly with Effect ChildProcess using shell mode derived from shouldUseShellForBinary in apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderHealth.ts lines 243-284. Missing-command detection there only looks at thrown ENOENT/notfound errors via isCommandMissingCause in lines 46-49, while nonzero exits are reported as "installed but failed to run" in the version-check branches at lines 302-338 and 510-546. On Windows, an unresolved codex or claude command under shell=true can return exit code 9009 instead of throwing, so Sam's Code will misclassify the CLI as present. The repo already has the exact Windows normalization helper in apps/server/src/processRunner.ts lines 40-53, but it is private and unused by ProviderHealth. The existing tests in apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderHealth.test.ts only cover spawn ENOENT for missing CLIs at lines 127-136 and 497-508, so the 9009 path is not already covered.

candidates.title: Add eol=lf to .gitattributes for Windows users (#1491)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds a root `.gitattributes` rule of `* text=auto eol=lf` to stop Windows Git checkouts from rewriting text files to CRLF.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same formatter sensitivity because the repo uses `oxfmt` for `bun fmt`, targets Windows desktop builds, and currently has no repository-level line-ending enforcement.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local evidence matches the upstream problem: `package.json` defines `"fmt": "oxfmt"`, `.oxfmtrc.json` exists, `.vscode/settings.json` sets the OXC formatter on save, but the repo has no `.gitattributes` and no `.editorconfig` `endOfLine` rule. `git ls-files --eol` shows blank `attr/` for tracked files and already reports some Windows working-tree drift (`.gitignore` and `LICENSE` as `w/mixed`, `stdout` as `w/crlf`). In this Windows environment, `git config --show-origin --get core.autocrlf` resolves to `true`, so adopting the upstream LF attribute would directly harden this fork against formatter churn and inconsistent checkouts.

candidates.title: fix(web): linkify Windows C:/ paths in terminal output (#1483)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream updates terminal path detection so Windows absolute paths written as C:/... are linkified, and it relaxes a WebSocket malformed-JSON test to assert only that a SyntaxError was logged instead of pinning the exact engine-specific message.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code uses the same terminal linkification flow in the desktop renderer, so this would make Windows forward-slash paths emitted in agent terminal output clickable and would remove one parser-message-specific test expectation.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local evidence shows the bug is still present: apps/desktop-renderer/src/terminal-links.ts:13-14 only matches Windows drive roots as [A-Za-z]:\\, not C:/, while the same module already treats both slash styles as absolute in isWindowsAbsolutePath at apps/desktop-renderer/src/terminal-links.ts:75 and resolvePathLinkTarget at apps/desktop-renderer/src/terminal-links.ts:158. That mismatch matters because apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx uses extractTerminalLinks and then opens matched paths in the editor, so C:/... paths from terminal output will currently not become clickable even though path resolution can handle them. The test side is also relevant locally: apps/desktop-renderer/src/wsTransport.test.ts:206-207 hardcodes the full SyntaxError text from invalid JSON, which is unnecessarily runtime-specific for a Bun/Vitest desktop renderer test when apps/desktop-renderer/src/wsTransport.ts:242 only needs to verify that malformed envelopes are dropped and logged.

candidates.title: Add thread archiving and settings navigation (#1359)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds thread archive and unarchive support across orchestration, persistence, and UI, plus settings-entry navigation and disabled desktop context-menu items needed to expose those actions safely.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already has settings navigation, but it still lacks any non-destructive thread lifecycle; porting the archive half would give the desktop sidebar a safer alternative to permanent deletion and fit the fork's session-centric UX.
candidates.recommendedReason: The local fork already wires settings access through the desktop menu and renderer route (`apps/desktop/src/main.ts` dispatches `open-settings`, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.tsx` listens for it, and `apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` is the actual settings page), so that upstream subfeature is effectively covered. What is missing is the archive model itself: local orchestration only defines `thread.create`, `thread.delete`, and related events with `deletedAt` but no `archivedAt` or archive/unarchive commands (`packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts`), the server projection table only persists `deleted_at` in `projection_threads` (`apps/server/src/persistence/Layers/ProjectionThreads.ts`), and the sidebar context menu only offers `Rename`, `Mark unread`, copy actions, and destructive `Delete` with a dedicated `confirmThreadDelete` setting (`apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/Sidebar.tsx`, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/appSettings.ts`). That concrete local shape means users can only keep threads active or permanently delete them today. Adapting the upstream archive flow would add real fork value without fighting the existing architecture, while the settings-navigation portion can mostly be treated as already satisfied.

candidates.title: Normalize typed provider runtime ingestion (#1475)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream removes legacy untyped payload parsing from provider runtime ingestion and treats `turn.completed` and `runtime.error` as fully typed events, with tests updated to normalize older fixture shapes.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already models provider runtime events as a typed schema union and its provider adapters emit typed `payload.state`, `payload.errorMessage`, and `payload.message`, so the same cleanup would reduce stale compatibility logic in the orchestration ingestion path.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local contracts make these fields typed today: `packages/contracts/src/providerRuntime.ts` defines `TurnCompletedPayload.state` and `RuntimeErrorPayload.message` as schema-backed payload fields, and both adapters emit them directly (`apps/server/src/provider/Layers/CodexAdapter.ts` around lines 535-538 and 713-722, `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts` around lines 1246-1253, 1369-1383, and 1452-1466). Despite that, `apps/server/src/orchestration/Layers/ProviderRuntimeIngestion.ts` still carries legacy helpers `runtimePayloadRecord`, `runtimeTurnState`, `runtimeTurnErrorMessage`, and `runtimeErrorMessageFromEvent`, and the `runtime.error` activity branch drops the activity entirely if that ad hoc extraction fails. That is out of step with the fork’s current typed runtime pipeline, which already reads these fields directly in `apps/server/src/upstreamSync/Layers/UpstreamSync.ts` lines 1090-1137. Applying the upstream normalization would make ingestion consistent with the local architecture rather than preserving compatibility for an event shape the fork no longer produces.

candidates.title: fix: trust node-pty install scripts on linux (#1451)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds `"trustedDependencies": ["node-pty"]` at the Bun workspace root so Bun will run `node-pty` install scripts, and records that in `bun.lock`.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code also depends on `node-pty` for its server-side terminal subsystem, so trusting that package at the root would protect Linux installs and builds for the fork's terminal feature.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local evidence matches the upstream intent: `apps/server/package.json` declares `"node-pty": "^1.1.0"`, `apps/server/src/terminal/Layers/NodePTY.ts` dynamically imports `node-pty` and explicitly looks for native artifacts under `build/Release` and `prebuilds/<platform-arch>/spawn-helper`, and the root `package.json` currently has no `trustedDependencies` entry. Since this repo uses Bun as the workspace package manager (`"packageManager": "bun@1.3.9"`) and ships Linux desktop artifacts via `dist:desktop:linux`, not trusting `node-pty` would risk broken native terminal support on Linux rather than being fork-specific noise.

candidates.title: Fix Linux desktop Codex CLI detection at startup (#1100)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream extends startup shell-environment hydration from macOS-only to Linux as well, adds platform-aware login-shell resolution, and ensures server PATH fixing runs before CLI-dependent startup checks.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same desktop-to-server startup chain and also resolves provider CLIs from inherited PATH, so Linux desktop launches can miss Codex detection unless PATH is hydrated from the login shell.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local evidence matches the upstream bug. `apps/desktop/src/main.ts` calls `syncShellEnvironment()` before spawning the backend, and `backendEnv()` forwards `...process.env`, but `apps/desktop/src/syncShellEnvironment.ts` currently returns unless the platform is `darwin`, so Linux desktop sessions never hydrate `PATH` or `SSH_AUTH_SOCK`. On the server side, `apps/server/src/main.ts` does call `cliConfig.fixPath` before startup, but `apps/server/src/os-jank.ts` is also `darwin`-only. That matters because `apps/server/src/codexAppServerManager.ts` resolves and spawns `codex` from `process.env`, and `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ProviderHealth.ts` probes `codex --version` via `resolveCliBinary("codex")`, so Linux GUI launches can report Codex missing even when it exists in the user's login shell PATH. `packages/shared/src/shell.ts` also lacks the upstream `resolveLoginShell` helper, so there is no Linux `/bin/bash` fallback when `SHELL` is unset.

candidates.title: fix(web): allow switching away from Ultrathink without manual prompt editing (#1307)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream changes the Claude effort picker so users can switch away from prompt-injected Ultrathink from the UI, and only blocks switching when "ultrathink" appears in the actual prompt body text.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same Claude effort control surface in the desktop renderer, including the compact composer menu path, so this fix would remove a local dead end without changing provider architecture.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local code still matches the pre-fix behavior. In apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/chat/ClaudeTraitsPicker.tsx, lines 77-78 set prompt-controlled state from isClaudeUltrathinkPrompt(prompt), line 110 immediately returns from handleEffortChange when that state is true, and lines 154-161 show "Remove Ultrathink from the prompt to change effort." while disabling every effort radio item. packages/shared/src/model.test.ts line 341 also asserts that body text like "Please ultrathink about this" triggers isClaudeUltrathinkPrompt, so Sam's Code still requires manual prompt editing for the exact scenario upstream fixed.

candidates.title: Fix server fallback to direct fd read on /proc/self/fd failure (#1488)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream makes server bootstrap resilient when duplicating an inherited fd through /proc/self/fd fails, falling back to reading directly from the inherited fd.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code boots its desktop server from environment variables and runs Codex app-server over plain stdio pipes, so this exact bootstrap-fd fallback path does not exist in the fork.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local startup uses env-based config in apps/server/src/main.ts via SAMSCODE_MODE, SAMSCODE_PORT, SAMSCODE_HOME, and SAMSCODE_AUTH_TOKEN, and apps/desktop/src/main.ts starts the backend with stdio set to either inherit or ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"]. Codex sessions in apps/server/src/codexAppServerManager.ts spawn "codex app-server" with stdio ["pipe", "pipe", "pipe"], consume child.stdout with readline, and write JSON-RPC to child.stdin. There is no local bootstrap.ts-style inherited-fd path: repo inspection found no resolveFdPath, readBootstrapEnvelope, or /proc/self/fd usage under apps/server/src, so the upstream fix does not match Sam's Code architecture.

candidates.title: fix(desktop): ensure all windows are destroyed before launching the NSIS installer during update process (#1461)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream hardens Windows auto-update install by destroying every Electron window before invoking the NSIS installer and by preventing the non-macOS `window-all-closed` handler from calling `app.quit()` once quitting is already in progress.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code uses the same `electron-updater` install path and ships a Windows NSIS target, so this would directly improve update reliability during installer handoff.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `apps/desktop/src/main.ts` still sets `isQuitting = true` and calls bare `autoUpdater.quitAndInstall()` in `installDownloadedUpdate()` around lines 833-846, with no pre-destroy of `BrowserWindow.getAllWindows()`. The same file also unconditionally calls `app.quit()` in `app.on("window-all-closed")` around lines 1584-1587, even while `isQuitting` is already true. That matches the upstream race the fix addresses. This fork also explicitly builds Windows with an NSIS target in `package.json` line 46 (`dist:desktop:win ... --target nsis`), so the upstream NSIS-specific installer behavior is relevant rather than hypothetical.

candidates.title: fix(web): add project path action to sidebar menu (#1436)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds a non-destructive project menu action to copy the selected project's filesystem path before the existing remove-project action.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same missing affordance in its desktop renderer project picker, and the fork already has reusable clipboard and context-menu primitives that make this a straightforward UX consistency win.
candidates.recommendedReason: The local equivalent lives in apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ProjectSelector.tsx, where handleProjectContextMenu currently shows only [{ id: "remove", label: "Remove project", destructive: true }] even though each Project carries a cwd and the dropdown already displays that path. The fork also already implements the same pattern for threads in apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/Sidebar.tsx, where the thread context menu includes "copy-path" and uses the reusable apps/desktop-renderer/src/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts hook. Because Sam's Code is an Electron desktop app with a nativeApi.contextMenu abstraction in packages/contracts/src/ipc.ts and apps/desktop-renderer/src/wsNativeApi.ts, the upstream intent maps cleanly to the fork's project selector architecture and is not already present.

candidates.title: fix: add noopener to external chat markdown links (#1315)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream hardens external chat markdown links by changing the fallback anchor rel from "noreferrer" to "noopener noreferrer".
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same chat-markdown external-link branch in the desktop renderer, so the upstream hardening applies directly to local chat, plan, and proposed-plan markdown surfaces.
candidates.recommendedReason: The local renderer still has the exact pre-fix code in apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatMarkdown.tsx, where non-file links return `<a ... target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" />` after `resolveMarkdownFileLinkTarget` rejects them. That component is reused by MessagesTimeline, PlanSidebar, and ProposedPlanCard, so this is not already covered. In this fork, `_blank` navigation is then handled by Electron in apps/desktop/src/main.ts via `window.webContents.setWindowOpenHandler` and `shell.openExternal`, which means ChatMarkdown is one of the remaining raw anchor-based external-link paths; adding `noopener` is a direct, low-risk hardening that matches the upstream intent.

candidates.title: Tighten settings nav and add Escape back navigation (#1503)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream shrinks typography in a dedicated settings sidebar nav and adds an Escape key handler that navigates back from the settings page.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code would only get partial value from the Escape shortcut, because the main UI change targets a settings navigation structure that does not exist in the fork.
candidates.recommendedReason: Sam's Code has no counterpart to upstream's SettingsSidebarNav: the local settings UI is a single long-form route in apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx with section cards under one header, and the app-wide shell still owns navigation via apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.tsx and apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/IconRail.tsx. The route shell already installs a window-level Escape handler in apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.tsx at line 60 to clear thread multi-selection, so porting the commit would mostly mean layering a settings-only window.history.back() listener into shared shell keyboard handling while the nav-tightening half has nowhere to apply.

candidates.title: feat: add mock update server (#1180)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds a localhost-backed mock desktop update feed for packaged builds and tightens updater install state handling so asynchronous install failures are surfaced without spurious action toasts.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same Electron auto-update surface, but it currently lacks any local mock update feed workflow and still treats install handoff as immediately complete.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local updater code still returns success immediately after `autoUpdater.quitAndInstall()` in apps/desktop/src/main.ts:833-849, with no `updateInstallInFlight` tracking anywhere in that file, so async installer failures can only hit the generic `autoUpdater.on("error")` path instead of being classified as install failures. The renderer also still toasts on any accepted incomplete action in apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/desktopUpdate.logic.ts:232-233, and its test still asserts `toasts only for accepted incomplete actions` in apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/desktopUpdate.logic.test.ts:158-174. On the tooling side, scripts/build-desktop-artifact.ts:197-239 and :470-473 only support GitHub publish metadata and default output to `release`, the repo has no `scripts/mock-update-server.ts`, no `start:mock-update-server` script in package.json, and no `release-mock` ignore entry in .gitignore. That means Sam's Code currently has no equivalent way to exercise updater manifests end to end; the upstream change is directly useful here, but should be adapted to the fork's `apps/desktop-renderer` paths and `SAMSCODE_*` env naming.

candidates.title: fix(ci): use whitespace-insensitive PR size diff (#1500)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream changes the PR-size workflow from GitHub's coarse PR file metadata to a checked-out git diff that ignores whitespace and blank lines and excludes test-only paths before assigning the size label.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code would only gain slightly more nuanced PR-size labels on formatting-heavy or test-only PRs, but the local workflow and repo layout do not match the main benefit of the upstream change.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local .github/workflows/pr-size.yml currently defines label descriptions as total changed lines and computes size directly from context.payload.pull_request.additions plus deletions, so its semantics are intentionally simple and match the label text. The upstream patch would change that meaning to a whitespace-insensitive, mostly non-test diff and would add checkout plus git fetch logic inside the privileged pull_request_target job. That complexity has little payoff here because a repo search found no local matches for the upstream test-focused paths and patterns such as apps/server/integration, __tests__, tests, *.test.*, or *.spec.*.

candidates.title: Fix sidebar thread panel weird states. (#1497)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream removes a nested `Collapsible` wrapper around each project’s thread sublist in the web sidebar and instead keeps the container mounted while conditionally rendering thread rows, which avoids odd thread-panel UI states when expanding or hiding project threads.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code also has a sidebar thread list with preview expansion, but its renderer uses a different architecture: project switching is separate from the thread list and the sidebar renders a flat list for only the active project.
candidates.recommendedReason: The upstream bug is tied to a per-project nested thread panel implemented with `Collapsible`/`CollapsibleContent`, but the local fork does not have that structure. In `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/Sidebar.tsx`, the sidebar derives one `activeProjectThreads` list at lines 746-761 and renders `visibleThreads.map(...)` directly at line 849 inside `SidebarMenu`; there is no `Collapsible` usage in that file and no `SidebarMenuSub` usage there either. Project switching is handled separately in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ProjectSelector.tsx` via `handleSelectProject` at line 63, and `_chat.tsx` mounts `ProjectSelector` in the title bar at line 243 while `ThreadSidebar` is a separate side panel at line 304. Because Sam's Code already avoids the combined project-header-plus-collapsible-thread-panel design that upstream fixed, there is no concrete local patch to apply from this commit.

candidates.title: fix: Prevent sidebar project dragging when using macos control+click to open context menu (#873)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds `isContextMenuPointerDown` and sidebar click-suppression so macOS control-click opens a project context menu without arming sortable drag or letting the follow-up click run.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code does have project context menus, but its project UI is a dropdown selector rather than a draggable sidebar project list, so the drag-prevention half of the change does not map to the fork.
candidates.recommendedReason: In the local fork, project selection lives in `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ProjectSelector.tsx`, where each project row is a plain button with `onClick={() => handleSelectProject(project.id)}` and `onContextMenu={(e) => void handleProjectContextMenu(e, project)}` around lines 272-281. A repo-wide search of `apps/desktop-renderer/src` found no `DndContext`, `useSortable`, `PointerSensor`, `dragInProgressRef`, or existing project-drag machinery, so the upstream fix's primary intent of stopping macOS control-click from arming sidebar drag is a concrete architecture mismatch for Sam's Code rather than a change to apply as-is.

candidates.title: Harden Claude stream exit handling (#1504)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream moves Claude stream-exit handling into the stream fiber itself so unexpected SDK termination always clears `context.streamFiber` and runs `handleStreamExit`, instead of depending on an observer callback to spawn cleanup afterward.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code uses the same Claude adapter pattern and its orchestration layer depends on timely `turn.completed` and `session.exited` runtime events to clear running session state.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts:2686-2695` still forks `runSdkStream(context)` directly and only calls `handleStreamExit` from `streamFiber.addObserver(...)`. The code that actually marks the Claude session stopped and removes it from the session map lives in `handleStreamExit` and `stopSessionInternal` (`apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts:2233-2265` and `apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts:2273-2320`), while `hasSession` reports any undeleted context as active (`apps/server/src/provider/Layers/ClaudeAdapter.ts:2842-2845`). Sam's Code's orchestration lifecycle then keys off `turn.completed` and `session.exited` to clear thread/session state (`apps/server/src/orchestration/Layers/ProviderRuntimeIngestion.ts:915-947` and `apps/server/src/orchestration/Layers/ProviderRuntimeIngestion.ts:1146-1148`). If Claude stream exit cleanup races or is skipped, the fork can retain a false-active Claude session and stale running state, so the upstream hardening is directly relevant.

candidates.title: feat(threads): auto-generate first-turn thread titles (#1375)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds automatic first-turn thread title generation by threading a new generateThreadTitle capability through contracts, server text-generation layers, and orchestration so new conversations get concise sidebar-safe names.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already gives first-turn threads automatic titles and also accepts later provider-driven thread renames, so the user-facing intent of this change is already covered in the fork.
candidates.recommendedReason: Sam's Code requires a title when creating a thread in packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts, and the renderer already derives that title from the first prompt, first image, or terminal context in apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/ChatView.tsx around the titleSeed and truncateTitle flow, then sends it via thread.create or first-turn thread.meta.update. The fork also maps provider thread.metadata.updated events back into thread.meta.update in apps/server/src/orchestration/Layers/ProviderRuntimeIngestion.ts lines 1175-1180. Upstream's server-side generateThreadTitle path would therefore duplicate an existing fork behavior, and it would also expand apps/server/src/git/Services/TextGeneration.ts beyond its current git-focused surface of commit, PR, branch, transcript, and upstream-sync generation.

candidates.title: Add keyboard shortcuts for jumping to sidebar threads (#1456)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream adds configurable thread navigation shortcuts (`thread.previous`, `thread.next`, and `thread.jump.1` through `.9`) plus renderer logic to resolve and display the effective shortcut for the visible sidebar thread list.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code does not have keyboard thread traversal today, and the upstream shortcut-resolution work would also fix misleading labels in the fork's existing context-sensitive keybindings.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local evidence shows the feature is absent and maps cleanly to the fork: `packages/contracts/src/keybindings.ts` only allows terminal/diff/chat/editor/speech commands, `apps/server/src/keybindings.ts` defines no thread-navigation defaults, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.tsx` only handles `chat.new` and `chat.newLocal` globally, and `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/Sidebar.tsx` navigates threads only via click/Enter on the active project's flat `visibleThreads` list. That active-project sidebar architecture is different from upstream's multi-project web sidebar, but it still gives a precise target for previous/next and 1-9 jump commands within the currently rendered thread list. Separately, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/keybindings.ts` still has the older `shortcutLabelForCommand` implementation that returns the last matching command label without evaluating `when` context or shortcut conflicts, even though local defaults in `apps/server/src/keybindings.ts` already overlap on `mod+d` and `mod+n`; the upstream resolver changes would improve label correctness for existing terminal and diff shortcuts as well.

candidates.title: Add new GitHub users to VOUCHED.td (#1508)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream appends a new GitHub username to `.github/VOUCHED.td`, expanding its maintainer vouch allowlist.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has release automation, but it does not use any `VOUCHED.td`-style maintainer trustlist; its release flow is driven by explicit GitHub Actions workflows and signing secrets instead.
candidates.recommendedReason: The local repo has no `.github/VOUCHED.td` file at all; `.github` contains only `workflows/` and issue templates. The actual fork release path is defined in `C:\Users\samsepi0l\Documents\Callpatch\Projects\samscode\.github\workflows\release.yml`, which performs tag-based builds and signing via secrets such as `AZURE_TRUSTED_SIGNING_*`, and `C:\Users\samsepi0l\Documents\Callpatch\Projects\samscode\.github\workflows\ci.yml` only runs quality and release smoke checks. Because Sam's Code does not implement upstream's GitHub-user vouch mechanism, adding one more username to that upstream-only allowlist has no local effect or corresponding integration point.

candidates.title: Truncate oversized git diffs instead of failing (#1499)
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream changes the server git runtime so oversized commit and range diff outputs are truncated with a marker instead of throwing, while preserving full checkpoint diffs.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code has the same large-diff failure path in AI commit and PR generation, and the upstream split between truncating prompt context but not checkpoint diffs matches the fork's server architecture.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local GitService still hard-fails when stdout/stderr exceeds 1,000,000 bytes (`apps/server/src/git/Layers/GitService.ts`), and `apps/server/src/git/Layers/GitCore.ts` still reads full staged patches in `prepareCommitContext` and full range patches in `readRangeContext` via `runGitStdout`. Those values are then immediately reduced in `apps/server/src/git/Layers/GitManager.ts` with `limitContext` before being sent to text generation, so large diffs can currently break commit-message or PR generation before the existing 50k/60k prompt limits are applied. This is not already present because `apps/server/src/git/Services/GitService.ts` only exposes `maxOutputBytes`, not the upstream truncation flag. The upstream scope also fits the fork cleanly: `apps/server/src/checkpointing/Layers/CheckpointStore.ts` gets checkpoint diffs through its own direct `git.execute` path, and `CheckpointReactor` treats checkpoint diff parsing as a separate concern, so keeping checkpoint diffs untruncated while truncating AI context diffs is the correct behavior for Sam's Code.

candidates.title: Update system overhaul (#1505)
candidates.summary:
Co-authored-by: Jono Kemball <jono-kemball@idexx.com>
Co-authored-by: Ariaj Sarkar <rajsarkar02205@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Julius Marminge <julius0216@outlook.com>
Co-authored-by: codex <codex@users.noreply.github.com>
candidates.changeSummary: Upstream overhauls desktop update UX by adding a separate check-result IPC contract, moving the sidebar update affordance into a footer pill, and surfacing update controls in settings with install confirmation copy.
candidates.forkValueSummary: Sam's Code already has manual update-check IPC plus a fuller desktop-renderer update surface, so the upstream reshuffle would mostly replace fork-specific UI patterns rather than add a missing capability.
candidates.recommendedReason: Local code already implements the core update-management path in a different architecture: `packages/contracts/src/ipc.ts:156` exposes `checkForUpdates(): Promise<DesktopUpdateState>`, `apps/desktop/src/main.ts:784` returns the current `DesktopUpdateState` from `checkForUpdates`, and `apps/desktop/src/main.ts:1354` already wires `desktop:update-check` for the renderer. On the UI side, `apps/desktop-renderer/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx:135-149` and `:1541-1568` already provide a dedicated settings panel with check/download/install actions, status, and release-note context, while `apps/desktop-renderer/src/components/Sidebar.tsx:623-648` and `:774-790` already keep a sidebar update trigger. Upstream's new `DesktopUpdateCheckResult` and footer-pill relocation are tied to its `apps/web` layout and would force unnecessary contract churn and UI replacement in the fork without solving a concrete Sam's Code gap.
